Project Manager II
description:
**Assiniboine Credit Union is hiring for the position of a permanent Full\-Time Project Manager II to work at the PMO \& Integration department.**


**About this opportunity:**


* This role requires to work 37\.5 hours per week, Monday to Friday.
* This role will be an hybrid position: some days working from home, and other days at the Head Office (540 St Anne's Rd, Winnipeg MB, R2M 5R7\).
* The position offers a competitive compensation plan, including health and dental benefits, matched pension, and 3 weeks of vacation.

  



Reporting to the Manager, Project Management, Project Manager II is a senior project management professional responsible for leading large\-scale, complex, and high\-impact initiatives that advances ACU’s strategic priorities. This role owns the delivery of multi\-stream projects with significant organizational risk, complexity, and interdependencies, requiring advanced judgment, autonomy, and stakeholder influence.



Project Manager II acts as a trusted advisor to leaders, enforces and evolves PMO standards, and mentors Project Manager I peers. Drawing on deep experience and PMI/PMBOK best practices, this role ensures disciplined delivery, strong governance, and sustained business outcomes aligned with Assiniboine Credit Union/ Caisse Assiniboine (ACU)’s commitment to rural and agricultural communities, francophone services, values\-based banking and ACU’s triple bottom line accountability to people, planet, and prosperity.

  


**Responsibilities:**

**Project Management:**

* Lead the end\-to\-end delivery of complex, cross\-functional projects, ensuring alignment with approved scope, schedule, budget, risk, quality, and dependencies.
* Own and manage project planning, governance, and delivery execution, exercising independent judgment to resolve issues and escalating only material risks.
* Provide executive\-level project reporting and decision support, including status, risk, change impacts, and recommendations.
* Lead cross\-functional project teams, fostering accountability, collaboration, and high performance.
* Ensure effective risk, issue, and quality management, including smooth transitions to operations, and measurement of post\-implementation outcomes and benefits realization.
* Oversee third\-party vendors and contractors; supporting contract negotiations, and ensure delivery and contractual compliance.
* Ensure all project documentation meets PMO standards and audit requirement; capture lessons learned to strengthen delivery practices.


**Business Analysis \& Stakeholder Engagement:**


* Act as a trusted advisor to business units, IT/Digital teams, and vendors to support informed decision making and solution delivery.
* Support complex business process analysis and redesign initiatives
* Oversee the translation of business needs into functional requirements and manage scope and changes throughout the solution lifecycle.
* Support build\-versus\-buy assessments and oversee user acceptance testing (UAT) to ensure solutions meet business needs
* Lead stakeholder engagement activities, including workshops and interviews to identify opportunities and drive effective change adoption.


**Continuous Improvement**


* Lead continuous improvement initiatives to enhance PMO processes, tools, and delivery practices.
* Drive the consistent adoption and evolution of project management standards across the organization.
* Mentor and coach Project Manager I peers, supporting PMO capability development and maturity.
* Apply lessons learned to improve future project delivery outcomes and overall value realization.

  


**EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE**



The competencies for the position would typically be acquired through 5\-7\+ years of progressive project management experience and a related post\-secondary degree or diploma, or an equivalent combination of education and experience. A Project Management Professional (PMP) designation is required.



Business Analysis certification (CBAP) and Organizational Change Management training are considered strong assets. Practical and operational business experience and knowledge within the financial services sector is required. Additional formal certifications are preferred.


**Key Occupational Skills**


* Strong understanding and application of PMBOK practices within regulated environments
* Ability to lead complex, multi\-stream projects, managing scope, schedule, budget, risk, quality, and dependencies
* Strong portfolio\-level judgment, including prioritization, sequencing, and trade\-off analysis
* Proven ability to lead and influence cross\-functional teams and senior stakeholders without direct authority
* Advanced stakeholder engagement and executive\-level communication skills
* Understanding of technological trends and the ability to apply them to delivery effective business solutions
* Ability to translate business needs into functional requirements and oversee solution validation and user acceptance testing (UAT)
* Strong analytical and systems thinking skills, with experience in business process redesign and solution enablement
* Demonstrated coaching and mentoring capability to support PMO maturity and peer development
* Experience managing third‑party vendors, including contract and performance oversight
* Advanced knowledge in quality control and assurance processes, particularly related to testing leading and facilitation
* Strong continuous\-improvement mindset, with the ability to strengthen project delivery standards, tools, and practices

  


**WHO WE ARE?**



As a Certified B Corporation® and one of Manitoba’s Top Employers (2024\), the culture at Assiniboine Credit Union / Caisse Assiniboine is supportive of the individual while focused on the greater community. With the widest geographic reach of any credit union in Manitoba, you’ll be motivated by shared values and encouraged to always think about how, through financial sustainability, we can do more for our members, our employees and our community.



Choosing to work at Assiniboine Credit Union means that you’ll be working for a uniquely Manitoban credit union that:


* puts people, planet and prosperity first,
* values diversity and inclusion,
* is committed to serving its urban, rural, agricultural, and Francophone communities,
* is focused on social and environmental responsibility,
* supports employees through training programs and long\-term career development,
* offers a competitive total compensation program which includes bonuses, a matched pension program, and health and dental benefits.


If doing things differently and doing more is part of who you are, join us on a rewarding journey where your personal aspirations and career goals can align.

  

**If you are interested in applying for the Project Manager II role, please submit your application and updated resume by Friday, April 10th, 2026, 4:00 pm.** We thank everyone who applies, but only candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.  

  

*We are committed to workforce diversity and actively recruit people with diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives reflecting the community in which we live and work. Applicants may request reasonable accommodation related to the materials and activities used throughout the selection process.*

description:
**Project Accounting Analyst**


**Winnipeg, MB**  

**Manitoba Hydro is consistently recognized as one of Manitoba's Top Employers!** We are a leader among energy companies in  

North America, recognized for providing highly reliable service and exceptional customer satisfaction. Join our team of Manitoba's  

best as we continue to build a company that champions safety, supports innovation, and delivers on our commitment to customer  

service \- while actively fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace reflective of the communities we serve.


***Great Benefits***


* Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
* Defined\-benefit pension plan for long\-term financial security.
* Nine\-day work cycle, typically resulting in every other Monday off to support a balanced approach to work, family life and



community.


***Position Overview:***



Under the general direction of the Generation Projects Financial Services Lead will be primarily responsible for providing project  

financial support on various construction projects managed by the Generation Project Management Department (GPD). This will  

include responsibility for providing support in monitoring and controlling expenditures and reviewing and processing  

invoices/progress statements.


***Responsibilities:***


* Review, analyze and/or prepare Invoices, Progress Statements, Miscellaneous Billing Requests and verify that payment


claims are valid and in compliance with the Terms and Conditions of payment. Evaluate/substantiate the accuracy and validity  

of backup documentation and make recommendations to Project Managers.

and progress statements for various contracts. Obtain appropriate signing authority with respect to release/approval.

of financial transactions, working with SAP cost reports and processing/parking journal entries.

inquiries/requests for additional information or analysis.

sent to Accounts Payable section to ensure supplier cheque receipt. Follow up and respond to late payment inquiries.  

* Provide training and guidance on the use of SAP for purchase requisition, service entries and project expenditure reports.
* Respond to internal and external customer inquiries with respect to contract progress claims, back\-up verification and


payments.

Payable and suppliers/contractors to expedite solutions to problems and respond to telephone and written inquiries related to  

the status of progress claims/payments.

role;

title: PROJECT ENGINEER
description:
**Project Engineer**


**Winnipeg, MB**  

**Manitoba Hydro is consistently recognized as one of Manitoba's Top Employers!** We are a leader among energy companies in  

North America, recognized for providing highly reliable service and exceptional customer satisfaction. Join our team of Manitoba's  

best as we continue to build a company that champions safety, supports innovation, and delivers on our commitment to customer  

service \- while actively fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace reflective of the communities we serve.


***Great Benefits***


* Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
* Defined\-benefit pension plan for long\-term financial security.
* Nine\-day work cycle, typically resulting in every other Monday off to support a balanced approach to work, family life and



community.


***Position Overview:***



We are seeking a Project Engineer to join our Generations Project Department. Under the guidance of the Dispatchable Capacity  

Project Manager, you will take a lead role in delivering high impact projects from concept to completion. You'll supervise and  

motivate a cross functional team while providing end to end project management across initiation, planning, execution, and close out  

phases.



In this role, you'll collaborate with multidisciplinary stakeholders to define project scope, develop realistic budgets and schedules,  

assess risk, and establish quality standards. You'll also shape resourcing and procurement strategies, and actively manage  

consultants, suppliers, contractors, and internal teams to ensure successful outcomes. This is an opportunity to drive complex  

projects forward, influence results, and contribute to meaningful, organization wide initiatives.


***Responsibilities:***


* Provide input or lead the development of project deliverables such as schedules, specifications, risk registers, cost estimates,


environmental and safety initiatives, etc.
* Responsible for coordination between the Project Management team, and multi\-disciplinary internal and external technical


groups, including planning, design, maintenance \& operations, engineering consultants and/or contractors.
* May supervise and mentor project staff including, staff officers, project specialists and Engineers.
* Lead, and provide input to, the development of CIJ (Capital Investment Justification) and CIJ Addendums including scope,



schedule, risk and cost estimates.  

* Provide project management expertise to support project participants regarding interdisciplinary engineering, financial, and


resource issues, and in consultation with stakeholders, establish project safety and environmental plans.  

* Lead, coordinate and/or participate in the management of Engineering consultants and/or contractors.
* Initiate, coordinate and direct the preparation of construction and supply tender documents, evaluations and


recommendations for contract award.  

* Ensure adequate performance information is provided to monitor, control and communicate critical issues and deadlines and


manage accountability with all Project Participants and stakeholders.  

* Responsible for the procurement, administration and close\-out for construction, supply and service contracts.
* Required to perform on\-site management of construction and commissioning activities in relief of the site Construction


Engineer or Contract Administrator occasionally.


***Qualifications:***


* Graduate in Engineering from a University of recognized standing, with a minimum of seven years related experience, of


which three years' experience must have been in project management, including three years of supervisory experience.
* Professional member in good standing with Engineers Geoscientists Manitoba (or willingness and ability to attain within a


specified amount of time).

MANITOBA HYDRO IS COMMITTED TO DIVERSITY AND EMPLOYMENT EQUITY


 Reference Code: CO57181152\-01


* Experience related to thermal generating facilities and formal project management training/education is an asset.
* Experience leading a team in delivering large capital projects, requiring specialized project management and planning is



considered an asset.  

* Experience in contract models for mega projects (Engineering, Procurement, and Construction/ Management,


Cost\-Reimbursable, Design Build, Construction Manager at Risk) for Large Projects is considered an asset.  

* Experience directing internal and external design and/or construction teams.
* Possess effective organizational and leadership abilities.
* Good computer skills including experience with scheduling software and the Microsoft Office suite of software.
* Strong organizational and leadership abilities.
* Mature judgment and proven ability to carry out assigned duties with a minimum of direction and supervision.
* Demonstrated high degree of initiative and ability to build positive and effective working relationships with others at all levels,


both inside and outside the corporation.  

* Must obtain and maintain a current Personal Risk Assessment and a "Clear" security rating in accordance with Manitoba


Hydro policy P513\.  

* Possess and maintain a valid Province of Manitoba Driver's Licence.

Medical Lab Technician - Tech 5 (AESES)
description:
Department of Human Anatomy and Cell Science
Rady Faculty of Health Sciences  

Position number: 34290
Date posted: April 1, 2026  

**Job details**
---------------


Medical Lab Technician \- Tech 5 (AESES)

Existing Regular Continuing Full\-Time (Budget Funded)



Full\-time: Yes
  

Permanent: Yes
  

Work schedule: 35 Hours/week (Monday to Friday, 8:30 am to 4:30 pm)
  

Salary: $28\.46 to $40\.88 per hour ($51,797\.20 to $74,401\.60 per annum)
  

Expected Start Date May 25, 2026
  

Trial/Probation period:
840 hours worked

  

For more information please contact: Jacki Armstrong \- jacki.armstrong@umanitoba.ca


**Qualifications**
------------------



**MINIMUM FORMAL EDUCATION/TRAINING:**



* Masters degree in Anatomical Science or a related study area with two years \= directly\-related experience.

OR
* Bachelors degree in a related discipline with five years \= directly\-related experience.

OR
* Completion of a Community College course in a related discipline with six years \= directly\-related experience.


**EXPERIENCE:**


* Detailed knowledge in regional human gross anatomy and neuroanatomy is required.
* Dissection skills and experience preparing cadaveric specimens are required.
* Experience in human anatomical dissection is required.
* Experience handling cadavers is required.
* Experience in anatomical image based computer programs is required.
* Experience in laboratory based anatomical teaching is preferred.
* Experience in clinical anatomical teaching is preferred.
* Experience in embalming techniques is preferred.
* Training in body embalming techniques is preferred.
* Basic understanding of educational concepts is preferred.
* An equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.

  




**SKILLS AND ABILITIES:**


* Excellent dissection skills are required
* Strong organizational skills are required.
* Demonstrated ability to self\-manage to meet deadlines is required.
* Excellent oral and written communication skills are required.
* Ability to work individually and as part of a team is required.
* Ability and willingness to learn new techniques is required.
* Excellent computer skills are required.
* Advanced computer skills, including experience with Windows/Apple/AV equipment, Microsoft Office, image databases, is required.
* Satisfactory work record, including satisfactory attendance and punctuality, is required.

  



**Key responsibilities**
------------------------



**INVOLVEMENT IN TEACHING PROGRAM:**



* Prepares detailed dissections of regional and whole body specimens for various teaching programs in RFHS and external groups.
* Introduces new dissections approaches in close consultation with the prosection lead.
* Prepares cadaveric specimens for special demonstration purposes using different embalmment techniques.
* Prepares material for neuroanatomy sessions using different techniques, including specific staining procedures for brain sections, and provides prosected neuroanatomy materials.
* Assists in demonstrating anatomical features of specimens for the training of learners in the Faculty of Health Sciences.
* Responsible for ensuring cleanliness of donor tables and demonstration areas during teaching sessions.
* Maintains donors preservation during demonstrations.
* Set up and cleaning of dissection tools for dissection lab.
* Works with lead prosector to select material for and set up class demonstrations and practical examinations for learners in the Faculty of Health Sciences.
* Assists in gross anatomy lab sessions for special educational events for internal and external users (e.g. resident’s training, surgical courses including cadaveric teaching). Occasionally, this may involve working outside of regular hours.
* Technician contact for all clinical training sessions i.e. communication pre\-session, coordinator for equipment drop\-off, contact during session etc.
* Prepares, dissects and maintains teaching specimens; i.e., preserves brain specimens in formalin, assembles, repairs and cleans articulated skeletons, maintains bone boxes and wax and plastic models to ensure that they are complete for teaching purposes in the gross anatomy lab and outside in a teaching class setting.
* Works closely with lead prosector to assist faculty members in providing learning material in online and distance education courses.
* Assists in setting up digital equipment and programs that will be used during teaching sessions for learners in the Faculty of Health Sciences as well as for special educational events for internal and external users.
* In the absence of the gross lab technician, set up and take down of anatomy lab sessions and assists faculty with same.
* In the absence of the gross lab technician, maintains cadaveric specimens in the lab.


**RECEIPT AND PREPARATION OF BODIES FOR TEACHING PURPOSES:**
* Acts as back up embalmer for Body Donation Program.
* Drains blood and embalms body using specialized techniques to provide deep preservation of cadavers for long term use.
* Maintains cadavers during long\-term storage.
* Maintains up\-to\-date computer records of all anatomical material in the morgue and in the dissecting room and ensures that this information is reconciled with the cadaver data maintained by Anatomy dept. administrative assistant responsible for the donation program.
* Assists with the selection of prosected material for teaching specimens prior to preparing bodies for cremation and burial.
* Reports on teaching related issues to the section head for gross anatomy and lead prosections and specimens.
  


**MAINTENANCE OF MORGUE AND DISSECTING AREAS:**



* Responsible for ensuring cleanliness in the morgue area, dissecting laboratories and demonstration areas.
* Prepares all chemicals for embalming, special injection dyes and stains for gross anatomy sections.
* Operates fork lift to move specimens in the storage area and on to the dissecting tables. Assists in maintaining all equipment assigned to the area to ensure their proper functioning. Ensures that safety procedures are followed when using equipment in the laboratory.
* Reports all necessary orders of supplies, chemicals, soaps, dissecting equipment for use in the morgue and dissecting areas to the laboratory manager.
* Reports any dysfunctional equipment and potential safety hazard in the morgue and dissection areas to the section head for gross anatomy.
* May be required to perform related duties not exceeding skills and capabilities as required.
**Additional information**
--------------------------

title: Quality Control & Quality Assurance Technician
description:
Join our team as a **Quality Control \& Quality Asusurance Technician** and play a key role in ensuring product quality and compliance.

**Primary Location:** 240\-555 Hervo St, Winnipeg, MB

**Primary Shift:** Monday to Friday, rotational shifts days (6:00 AM – 2:30 PM) \& evenings (3:30 PM – 12:00 AM). Some weekends may be required based on business needs.

Our People Can Tell the Future. Maybe You Can Too.

***Who are we?***

We are a warehousing and transfer company that brings pride and excellence to the market every day. We bring innovation that’s authentic and measurable as well as bringing leadership to community priorities in equal unison with our corporate priorities. We recognize our core strength is ingenuity, which is a human resource that renews through encouragement and development. We are twenty\-first century accomplishment leaders; inside, safety side and market side.

ISO 9001:2015 certified; Future Transfer is an essential network of specialist services for an agriculture sector with sophisticated challenges. Strategic national warehousing, next level logistics, tolerance specific custom formulation, packaging, transloading, and a fully inclusive company\-wide think\-tank of empowered solution achievers.

Future Transfer is a growing story; join us and tell your part.

***Why work for us?***

* Competitive wages
* Premium health benefits
* Group Retirement Plan (RRSP/DPSP)
* Employee Assistance Program
* Company supplied uniforms, PPE \& safety shoes
* Short term disability and long\-term disability
* Continued growth and development of our people \& our business

***Summary:***

The QC\&QA Technician is responsible for receiving, labeling, and testing samples and products formulated in the facility. This role involves operating various laboratory instruments, performing quality control (QC) and quality assurance (QA) tests, and ensuring compliance with Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and safety regulations.

***Job Duties:***

* Conduct various laboratory testing procedures in accordance with SOPs to verify product quality and compliance.
* Handle and operate sensitive laboratory instruments, including but not limited to: Gas Chromatography (GC)), Liquid Chromatography (LC, HPLC), pH meters, Analytical balances, Viscometers, Titration equipment, Moisture analyzers (if applicable), Density meters and Karl Fischer Titrators.
* Perform sample preparation, testing, and data interpretation with precision and accuracy.
* Record all test results and analytical data in the designated formats.
* Maintain and update equipment records and daily laboratory logbooks.
* Ensure proper calibration, performance verification, and routine maintenance of laboratory instruments according to established procedures.
* Assist with method validation, verification, and laboratory procedure updates under supervision.
* Perform laboratory supplies inventory and communicate the needs to the supervisor.
* Review customer manuals, product specifications, and other documented customer requirements prior to the start of each production campaign to ensure product quality and compliance with agreed specifications.
* Prepare, review, update, and issue production documentation, including packaging batch sheets and formulation/blend sheets, ensuring proper version control and alignment with approved formulations and quality requirements.
* Review production batch records, test reports, certificates of analysis (COA), and other quality documents for completeness, accuracy, and compliance with internal procedures and regulatory requirements.
* Support QA documentation activities including document control, SOP creation and updates, quality records management, and controlled document distribution.
* Participate in internal quality audits, compliance checks, and continuous improvement initiatives related to laboratory and quality systems.
* Assist in investigations of non\-conforming results, and quality deviations, including documentation of findings and corrective actions.
* Ensure laboratory compliance with applicable quality systems and standards such as Croplife and AWSA standards and ISO 9001 Quality Management System.
* Follow all laboratory safety protocols, including proper handling of corrosive, flammable, and hazardous chemicals.
* Maintain a clean, organized, and efficient laboratory environment in accordance with company policies.
* Collaborate with the QA/QC team and production staff to address quality\-related concerns.
* Perform other duties related to quality control testing, quality assurance documentation, and laboratory operations as required.

***Required Knowledge/Experience:***

* College Diploma or University Degree in Chemistry, Biochemistry, or a Lab Technician Program (Postgraduate degrees will be given preference).
* Minimum 1 year of experience in a laboratory and/or industrial environment.
* Understanding of laboratory quality control programs and compliance requirements.
* Ability to operate, maintain, and troubleshoot laboratory instruments.
* Strong analytical mindset with the ability to follow SOPs precisely.
* Proficiency in MS Office (especially Excel) for data analysis; strong mathematical knowledge is an asset.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills for reporting and collaboration.
* Ability to prioritize tasks, manage multiple responsibilities, and meet deadlines in a fast\-paced environment.
* A proactive and trustworthy individual who can work independently and within a team.

***Working Conditions:***

* Given the nature of production, shift work may be required during peak seasons, depending on production schedules.
* Work is performed in both a laboratory and a busy plant facility.
* Prolonged periods of standing and conducting tests may be necessary.
* Must be able to lift at least 25 lbs occasionally.
* Overtime, as required

*Future Transfer is an equal opportunity employer. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit and business need. We are committed to providing an environment of mutual respect where equal employment opportunities are available to all applicants and team members.*

Safety is our priority, both the starting point and foundation for all aspects of our business operations. Safe transport and storage of products, safe operational practices and safe working conditions enable us to protect our employees, customers, suppliers, and the environment.

To read more about our successes and what we do, please visit: www.futuretransfer.com

We thank you for your interest in careers at Future Transfer.

The organization also delivers some province\-wide health services and supports centralized administrative and business functions for Manitoba health organizations.

**Position Overview**
---------------------



Under the general supervision of the Charge or designate, the General Duty Medical Laboratory Technologist is responsible for clinical analysis and reporting on patient samples according to established protocols. The General Duty Medical Laboratory Technologist is also responsible for assisting in general lab projects such as SOPs, chemical inventories, etc. The incumbent will maintain a safe environment in accordance with professional and provincial guidelines and ensure compliance with quality assurance programs and maintain standards put forth by Accreditation. ----------------------------------------

**Qualifications and Skills**
-----------------------------


* Must be able to communicate effectively in English, both written and oral.
* Windows computer environment required.
* Microsoft Office programs preferred.
* Follow oral and written instructions precisely with attention to detail.
* Demonstrated ability to work both independently and as a team member.
* Proficient analytical skills.
* Work under pressure.
* Maintains confidentiality with all information entrusted to the position.
* Fluent language skill (written and oral) in both French may be required.
* Ability to perform duties as described.
**Physical Requirements**
-------------------------


* Working shifts, weekends and holidays may be required.
* Participating in standby and callback duty may be required.
* Rotate through various workstations as required.
* Exposure to all hazards and dangers incumbent in working in a laboratory.
* For more definitive information, refer to the Job Hazard Analysis Assessment of this position within the specific laboratory.

**This position requires a current satisfactory Criminal Records Check (including Vulnerable Sector Search), Child Abuse Registry Check and Adult Abuse Registry Check as conditions of employment. The successful candidate will be responsible for any service charges incurred. A security check is considered current if it was obtained no more than six (6\) months prior to the start of employment.**

title: Senior Project Manager
description:
**The future you want is within reach. Let's build it together.**


At PCL Constructors Canada Inc., part of the PCL Family of Companies (PCL), we don't just build projects \- we build opportunities, careers and communities. We are 100% employee\-owned, every employee has a stake in our success, and that shared commitment drives how we work, grow and lead in the construction industry.

  

We're a team of builders who care deeply about what we create and who we build with. That includes you. We are not only shaping the future of construction, we are also committed to shaping the future of your career.

  

As a Senior Project Manager for PCL Constructors Canada Inc. in Winnipeg, Manitoba, you'll have the opportunity to provide strategic leadership across complex projects, aligning teams that shape our communities.

 **Why Choose PCL?**

  

Choose a career with rewards that matter. PCL's total rewards are designed to support your growth, well\-being and future success \- because when you succeed, we all do. Our offerings could include:

  

* Employee ownership opportunities that build long\-term value
* Annual discretionary performance bonuses
* RRSP, TFSA, Pension Contribution Options
* Flexible medical, dental and vision benefits
* Prescription drug coverage and virtual care services
* Life, AD\&D and disability insurance
* Paid parental leave and family care support
* Health and lifestyle spending account options
* Mental health and wellness support, including Employee Assistance Programs
* Career growth pathways, leadership development and mentorship programs
* Access to world\-class training through PCL's College of Construction and professional development courses
* Ongoing opportunities to learn new skills, explore different roles and grow your career across sectors and regions

  

Here's how a Senior Project Manager for PCL Constructors Canada Inc. within Winnipeg contributes to our team:

**Responsibilities**
--------------------

* Develops, implements, and maintains the project safety program for a safe work environment. Creates and influences safety culture as a mentor and model.
* Conducts job site tours and safety inspections and leads by example to ensure that policies, procedures, environmental programs and safety standards are followed.
* Conducts site and safety inspections, attends progress meetings, leads by example, reviews incidents, near misses, HSE reports, and statistics, and effects change to ensure that project safety standards, legislation, and environmental programs are followed.
* Liaises with clients to ensure that projects are constructed as per quality and safety standards, and contract.
* Leads to secure new work by participating in estimates, working with trade contractors, and/or participating in proposals and presentations.
* Oversees the development and implementation of quality management plan and quality culture with stakeholder buy\-in.
* Understands client drivers and offers value engineering proposals for functionality, cost, and/or schedule benefit to the client while maintaining value\-added outcomes as part of our solution provider service where warranted.
* Develops or oversees the Project Execution Plan (PEP) with the superintendent.
* Coordinates, monitors, and manages design deliverable schedule to facilitate and/or assist with permits, procurement, shop drawings, off\-site production, construction and commissioning in conjunction with the review and signoff by the client.
* Conducts review of design and seeks resolution from the design team with respect to compliance, scope creep, cost optimization, code requirements, procurement, safety in design, construction sequencing, and constructability.
* Conducts review of design documentation, leveraging the necessary team members, and seeks resolution from the design team with respect to level of design completeness, accuracy, and quality as applicable to various design stages and construction.
* Oversees and monitors project performance status reports for schedule, costs, equipment, manpower, materials, safety and environment, cost forecast, and quality control, and programs.
* Monitors production and reviews work of assigned resources to ensure that project time, cost, and quality objectives are met.
* Identifies scope gaps, technical risk, and areas requiring interdisciplinary coordination from the design documents.
**Qualifications**
------------------

* Postsecondary education in engineering, construction management, or a related discipline.
* Gold Seal certification preferred.
* 15\+ years of progressive construction experience with multiple contract delivery types as a leader and mentor.
* 12 months of estimating or related experience preferred.
* In\-depth knowledge and application of safety and environment principles/ procedures/legislation.
* In\-depth knowledge of construction industry and company operations, including building materials, construction specifications, methods and procedures, safety and quality management practices for large, complex, and more diverse projects.
* Experience with diverse project types, delivery and construction methods, and sequencing and timelines.
* Strong knowledge of and ability to apply project construction laws and building codes and to leverage such knowledge.
* Understanding of and experience with collective agreements and jurisdiction assignment for multi\-trade sites.
* Thorough understanding of insurance, bonding, and legal requirements.
* Effective verbal, written, presentation, and communication skills.
* Demonstrated flexibility and ability to work effectively in a fast\-paced environment.
* Ability to monitor and forecast project resources and staffing for the project.
* Ability to identify staffing and hourly labor issues.
**Your Work Has Purpose Here**

  

PCL projects are where life happens, where communities connect, careers begin, and progress is made. Regardless of the type of project, we are building something bigger: a future that's inclusive, resilient and full of opportunity.

  

At PCL Constructors Canada Inc., we are committed to creating a workplace where everyone belongs. We value the diverse experiences, identities and perspectives our employees bring. Employment decisions are based on merit, potential and the drive to make a difference, regardless of race, gender, age, ability or background.

  

We know everyone's needs are different, if you require accommodation during the application process, please contact careers@pcl.com and include the position and location of interest.

  

This recruitment process does not involve the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to screen, assess, or select applicants. All hiring decisions are made by human reviewers.

 **Company:** PCL Constructors Canada Inc.

**Primary Location:** Winnipeg, Manitoba

**Job Title:** Senior Project Manager

**Requisition ID:** 11809

Shared Health leads the planning and coordinates the integration of patient\-centered clinical and preventive health services across Manitoba. The organization also delivers some province\-wide health services and supports centralized administrative and business functions for Manitoba health organizations.

**Position Overview**
---------------------



Under the general supervision of the Charge or designate, the General Duty Medical Laboratory Technologist is responsible for clinical analysis and reporting on patient samples according to established protocols. The General Duty Medical Laboratory Technologist is also responsible for assisting in general lab projects such as SOPs, chemical inventories, etc. The incumbent will maintain a safe environment in accordance with professional and provincial guidelines and ensure compliance with quality assurance programs and maintain standards put forth by Accreditation. Functions are conducted in accordance with the Mission and Values of Shared Health Diagnostic Services, current policies, procedures and applicable legislation.


**Experience**
--------------


* Previous related experience preferred.
**Education (Degree/Diploma/Certificate)**
------------------------------------------


* Graduate of an approved Medical Laboratory Technology program.
* Successful completion of the CMLTM Prior Learning Assessment process for a discipline specialty, as deemed by the Employer may also be considered.
**Certification/Licensure/Registration**
----------------------------------------


* Current registration or eligible for registration with the College of Medical Laboratory Technologists of Manitoba (CMLTM) required.
* Current membership with the Canadian Society for Medical Laboratory Science (CSMLS) considered an asset.
**Qualifications and Skills**
-----------------------------


* Must be able to communicate effectively in English, both written and oral.
* Windows computer environment required.
* Microsoft Office programs preferred.
* Follow oral and written instructions precisely with attention to detail.
* Demonstrated ability to work both independently and as a team member.
* Proficient analytical skills.
* Work under pressure.
* Maintains confidentiality with all information entrusted to the position.
* Fluent language skill (written and oral) in both French may be required.
* Ability to perform duties as described.
**Physical Requirements**
-------------------------


* Working shifts, weekends and holidays may be required.
* Participating in standby and callback duty may be required.
* Rotate through various workstations as required.
* Exposure to all hazards and dangers incumbent in working in a laboratory.
* For more definitive information, refer to the Job Hazard Analysis Assessment of this position within the specific laboratory.

  





We have a unique ability to work together to make health care better. If you want to make a difference and contribute to supporting the health of your family, friends and neighbours, please apply today.

Research Assistants are normally students, in a University of Winnipeg undergraduate program, employed to assist with tasks related to research. Work is performed within defined guidelines and under supervision of a more senior researcher or faculty member.

description:
Senior Research Assistants are normally graduate students, or University of Winnipeg undergraduate students, employed to assist in the execution and evaluation of research projects in their area of study. The positions may oversee Research Assistants in areas such as the use of proper techniques, analysis, and documentation.

description:
Who We Are
  
Price Industries Limited, North America’s leading manufacturer and distributor of air distribution products (www.priceindustries.com). The founding principles of Price have never changed – business integrity, first\-class service, innovation and a commitment to people. Our dependable quality, reliable delivery, and knowledgeable follow\-up service have made Price a market leader in supplying air distribution, critical controls, and noise control products.
  
After more than 60 years, Price remains a privately held family company with a deep heritage and commitment to innovation and service. Our long\-standing vision, values, and the Price way of doing business are the cornerstones of our success.
  
We are a dynamic, progressive and innovative organization looking for new talent in our growing Winnipeg manufacturing facility. If you thrive on new challenges, and working in a friendly, team\-driven environment, you should consider the following opportunity.
  
What You Can Expect
  
* Health and Dental \- Employees and their families enjoy paid benefits covering an extensive list of medical and dental expenses and services.
* Employee and Family Assistance Program (EFAP) \- You and your family will have free access to consultation and support for your mental, emotional and financial well\-being
* PocketPills – You and your family will have free access to Canada’s first Digital Pharmacy, PocketPills enables you to call, text or email the pharmacy care team from wherever you are. You will enjoy free delivery of your prescriptions and vitamins straight to your home.
* Life and Disability \- You will be provided with life insurance in a multiple of your salary with an option to purchase additional coverage.
* Pension Matching Program \- Invest in your future with each pay cheque through our Defined Contribution Pension Plan and we will match your contribution (up to 3% of your salary).
* Gym Subsidy Program \- Employees are eligible for a gym membership reimbursement of up to $300 per year, this includes Fitness related App’s.
* As part of our Goals, Rewards, Improvement and Teamwork program qualifying teams can enjoy a monthly reward.
* Personal and Professional Development \- We are committed to helping you reach your potential through training and education.
* Community Involvement \- Generously supporting health, charities, youth, and education opportunities is our passion, and you’re invited to be a part of that.
* Our convenient Price Café offers free Tea and Coffee.


**Reporting to:** Sales Technology Leadership Team
  
You will play a vital role in ensuring the seamless operation and continuous improvement of our web\-based Sales Portal, supporting external users. This position focuses on day\-to\-day monitoring, service requests, and incident resolution while supporting various operational areas across the company. The ideal candidate brings strong application knowledge, troubleshooting expertise, and process analysis capabilities.
  
  

**The successful candidate’s responsibilities will include:**  

* Provide application support for the company’s Sales Portal and other applications, including:
* Data maintenance
* User account administration
* Daily monitoring
* Incident management
* Request fulfillment
* Root cause analysis
* Ensure compliance with internal and external security standards by:
* Following policies and procedures
* Performing periodic reporting
* Conduct functional testing and maintain documentation to support new solution readiness
* Support continuous improvement by:
* Assisting developers and analysts with enhancements
* Triaging tickets and collaborating with Product Leads and Customer Success teams
* Maintain and update knowledge base articles and work instructions
* Communicate effectively with supported business areas


**The ideal candidate for this position will have:**  

* 3 to 5 years of application support experience in a SaaS, user\-software, or manufacturing environment
* Post\-secondary education in Computer Science, Information Systems, or equivalent


Technical Skills
  
* Strong knowledge of large\-scale SaaS platforms
* Experience with troubleshooting methodologies and root cause analysis
* Working knowledge of:
* Programming languages
* Relational databases
* SharePoint, Progress, and SQL
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office


Assets
  
* Experience with Azure DevOps
* ITIL Certification
* Knowledge of financial/accounting processes in manufacturing
* Experience creating technical documentation and training materials


Core Competencies
  
* Strong customer service mindset
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills
* Ability to prioritize in a fast\-paced environment
* High attention to detail and accuracy
* Self\-motivated with the ability to work independently and collaboratively


Should this excellent opportunity interest you, please submit your resume, including salary expectations.
  
  

Price Industries Limited hires on the basis of merit and is strongly committed to equity and diversity. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates, including all genders, Indigenous peoples, persons with disabilities, members of visible minorities, individuals of diverse gender and sexual orientation and all groups protected by the Human Rights Code. We are happy to provide reasonable accommodations throughout the selection process and while working at Price. If you require support applying online because you are a person with a disability, please contact us at HR@priceindustries.com .
  
  

We appreciate the interest shown by all applicants, however only those being considered for an interview will be contacted.

description:
**Business Systems Analyst**


**Winnipeg, MB**  

**Manitoba Hydro is consistently recognized as one of Manitoba's Top Employers!** We are a leader among energy companies in  

North America, recognized for providing highly reliable service and exceptional customer satisfaction. Join our team of Manitoba's  

best as we continue to build a company that champions safety, supports innovation, and delivers on our commitment to customer  

service \- while actively fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace reflective of the communities we serve.


***Great Benefits***


* Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
* Defined\-benefit pension plan for long\-term financial security.
* Nine\-day work cycle, typically resulting in every other Monday off to support a balanced approach to work, family life and



community.


***Position Overview:***



Under the general direction of the supervisor, the incumbent leads analysis and requirements modeling for a functional domain  

across integrated applications primarily within the Work Management Applications domain. Responsible for end\-to\-end business  

analysis activities including requirements elicitation, documentation, configuration oversight, and testing coordination for moderately  

complex changes and upgrades. Provides consultative advice to business clients, mentors junior team members, and participates in  

planning and execution of technology initiatives.


***Responsibilities:***


* May lead and perform configuration for moderately complex changes and enhancements; reviews work of junior analysts and


ensures quality and compliance with established processes.
* May lead and perform requirements modelling (process maps, use case narratives, data flows); conducts walkthroughs for


validation and sign\-off.
* Mentors junior analysts by reviewing deliverables, sharing best practices, and providing feedback to support skill


development.
* May lead and author comprehensive test plans; prepares test data/cases; coordinates and executes system and user


acceptance testing.
* May lead and contribute to assigned work packages within IT software projects, upgrades, or initiatives; provides input into


planning, design, and execution activities.
* May lead and perform consulting for complex requirements; analyzes options and advises on functional approaches that align


with business objectives and technology standards.
* May lead and perform resolution of complex functional issues across integrated applications and interfaces; develops


knowledge base articles and supports operational procedures.
* Partners with BI and analytics teams to design and validate advanced dashboards and reporting solutions; provides input on


data models and ensures alignment with business processes.
* May lead and perform impact assessments for industry trends and system updates; recommends adoption strategies for


assigned domains.
* May lead and perform day\-to\-day dealings with vendors for assigned systems; evaluates proposals, reviews deliverables, and


provides recommendations to stakeholders.
* May lead and perform the development and delivery of end\-user training for system changes and upgrades; coordinates


training logistics and ensures materials meet business needs.
* Leverages enterprise tools including SAP, ServiceNow, Azure DevOps, and reporting platforms to lead analysis activities,


coordinate testing, and support delivery of functional changes across assigned areas.

corporate standards and vendor agreements.
* Plans and executes upgrades for supported solutions; performs critical steps, assesses impacts and ensures alignment with


integration and security standards.
* Participates in the development of departmental strategies and plans; recommends initiatives to improve system performance

description:
**Job Posting: Program Coordinator**  
**Location:** North East Winnipeg  
**Organization:** S.P.I.K.E. Inc.  
**Employment Type:** Full\-Time

S.P.I.K.E. Inc. is seeking a dedicated and organized Program Coordinator to join our team in North East Winnipeg. Reporting to the Program Manager, this position is responsible for the day\-to\-day operations of our client care programs, ensuring services are delivered in line with individualized care plans, financial goals, and regulatory standards.

This role plays a critical part in ensuring the well\-being of our clients while supporting staff and maintaining program efficiency. If you’re passionate about making a difference and have strong leadership, communication, and problem\-solving skills, we encourage you to apply.

Key Responsibilities

**Client Care and Well\-Being**

* Oversee the physical, emotional, and social well\-being of all clients.
* Ensure care plans are current, followed, and tailored to each individual.
* Work closely with clients, families, and interdisciplinary teams to address needs.
* Monitor progress and adjust care plans as necessary.
* Support clients in achieving personal goals and enhancing independence.
* Ensure clients are enrolled in and maintaining essential benefits such as OAS, Pharmacare, and Manitoba Supports.

**Financial and Care Plan Management**

* Maintain and update financial and care plans in compliance with policies and standards.
* Ensure adherence to client care budgets and proper allocation of resources.
* Monitor expenditures and promote cost\-effective practices.
* Fulfill reporting requirements related to service agreements with CLDS and RCL.
* Submit regular financial and care plan updates to management.
* Obtain quotes for special purchases or emergencies up to $500\.

**Staff Supervision and Development**

* Supervise and support staff in the homes, ensuring quality service delivery.
* Conduct performance evaluations and assist with professional development.
* Ensure staff are trained and up to date on care practices and safety protocols.
* Assist with scheduling and conflict resolution.
* Lead staff meetings and foster a collaborative work environment.

**Compliance and Reporting**

* Maintain accurate, confidential records in accordance with FIPPA, PHIA, and The Adults Living with an Intellectual Disability Act.
* Submit regular reports on client care, staff performance, and operations.
* Monitor and report on residence maintenance and safety.
* Audit daily operations to ensure efficiency and regulatory compliance.

**Collaboration and Communication**

* Serve as a point of contact for clients, staff, and families.
* Work with external service providers and community resources to support client needs.
* Participate in organizational committees, such as health and safety or pension planning.

**Other Duties**

* Perform additional tasks and projects as assigned by management.

**Requirements:**

* Valid Class 5F MB driver’s license
* Clear Criminal Record Check with Vulnerable Sectors Search
* Clear Adult and Child Abuse Registry Checks
* First Aid and CPR Level C
* NVCI Certification and ASL (asset)

title: Recreation Coordinator
description:
**Job Title:** Recreation Coordinator  
**Pay Level:** $22\.50  
**Reports To:** Program Manager

**Position Summary:**  
We are looking for a motivated Recreation Coordinator to design, coordinate, and deliver engaging recreational programs that enhance the lives of the people we support. This role promotes meaningful engagement, community involvement, wellness, and personal growth. The Recreation Coordinator will lead a variety of activities, organize outings and events, maintain safe transportation, and actively support participants’ day\-to\-day well\-being.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* **Program Planning \& Delivery:** Develop and lead activities including physical, creative, cultural, recreational, social, spiritual, and community service programs. Adjust plans as needed to meet participant interests and abilities.
* **Participant Engagement:** Meet with new participants to identify interests, encourage attendance, and support day\-to\-day participation. Maintain accurate participation records.
* **Community \& Event Coordination:** Organize outings, social events, and seasonal activities; liaise with families, community organizations, and external partners; safely transport participants.
* **Administration \& Communication:** Prepare monthly recreation calendars and newsletters, manage activity spaces, and maintain supplies and resources.
* **Staff Collaboration \& Leadership:** Support and instruct staff in activity facilitation, collaborating with management to deliver high\-quality programs.
* **Budget \& Compliance:** Track activity budgets, maintain financial records, and ensure compliance with organizational policies.
* **Other Duties:** Maintain required certifications (nutrition, insulin, diabetic training), and perform other related duties as assigned.

**Qualifications:**

* Excellent interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills in English.
* Strong organizational, problem\-solving, and time management skills.
* Creative, proactive, and able to contribute ideas for new programs.
* Professional, ethical, and able to work independently or collaboratively.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint; Adobe software is a plus.

**Education \& Licensing:**

* Diploma in Recreation Therapy or related field is an asset.
* Valid Class F or I Driver’s License and current driver’s abstract required.

**Experience:**

* Previous experience in recreation, wellness, or supportive services preferred.

Senior Project Manager – Bridges and Structures
description:
When faced with an impasse, do you look to bridge the gap? Our Bridges Team does too. From local bridge inspection and rehabilitations that support our communities to iconic bridge designs that define a region, our opportunities will connect you to your career goals.


A career in transportation is more than bridges, roads, and rail. As we plan, design, and manage projects that move people and goods from Point A to Point B, we create connections that transform our communities. Every airport, road, bridge, port, or transit project is an opportunity to make communities safer, cleaner, and more resilient. Together, we’re making an impact around the world.


If you’re looking for a career that moves, join our team of professionals who are passionate about the future of transportation.


Your Opportunity


An opportunity exists for a senior Bridge Engineer to become part of our dynamic Transportation Engineering group in Manitoba. This position will be full\-time in\-office or hybrid based in our Winnipeg office. Stantec’s Transportation Team has a strong local presence but also participates in some of Stantec’s largest and most complex transportation projects. These projects encompass the entire life of the transportation structure – from feasibility studies through detailed design and construction oversight. Are you excited by and passionate about this interesting and challenging field of engineering?


Your Key Responsibilities


* Project management and/or design management of bridges, culverts, retaining walls and foundation engineering assignments.
* Managing change in scope, schedule, risks and costs against the project baseline.
* Interaction with project controls and associated reporting.
* Regularly interface with the client regarding project status updates and expectations.
* Account and stakeholder management maximizing business opportunities within client accounts.
* Analysis, design, preparation of tender packages, visual inspections, report preparation, cost estimating, construction engineering services.
* Proposal preparation and assisting to develop client relations.
* Development of structural rehabilitation, strengthening, and replacement strategies.
* Leading the development of contract drawings, design details, specifications, cost estimates, and reports for various bridge types and other transportation structures.
* Undertaking bridge, culvert, and other structural inspections to assess condition.
* Mentoring junior bridge engineers and bridge technologists.
* Regular collaboration with colleagues in our multi\-disciplined design office.
* Coordination with sub\-consultants.
* Construction administration and inspection.

Your Capabilities and Credentials


* Demonstrated ability to organize, coordinate, and project manage quality, budget, schedule, and technical deliverables for bridge projects at all phases of the project life cycle.
* Strong client management skills.
* Proven understanding of projects controls systems and cost control practices.
* Advanced understanding of structural design and analysis concepts and methods.
* Working knowledge of relevant bridge design codes, particularly CHBDC.
* Familiarity with City of Winnipeg and Manitoba Transportation and Infrastructure bridge design criteria, construction specifications, inspection and construction administration manuals, and standard drawings.
* Experience with structural modelling and design software such as SAP2000, CSiBridge, or equivalent is an asset.
* Ability to create and interpret sketches, drawings, and other documentation associated with a typical variety of structures.
* Ability to participate and collaborate in project team setting, to engage in creative and critical thought, and to effectively communicate concepts and ideas to others.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills.
* Enthusiastic attitude with interest in skills development and continual improvement.
* Ability to work independently and in a team environment.
* Proficient in Microsoft (MS) Office Suite.
* Valid Class 5 Manitoba Driver’s License.

Education and Experience


* Bachelor’s degree in civil engineering focusing on structural engineering, with preference for individuals with a Master’s degree.
* Must be a Licensed Professional Engineer
* Minimum of 15 years of Bridge\-related work experience.

While Stantec offers hybrid work options, this position will primarily work in an office setting and will require some travel and field work to other office locations as well as project locations.


This description is not a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that may be required of the employee and other duties, responsibilities and activities may be assigned or may be changed at any time with or without notice.


Stantec is a place where the best and brightest come to build on each other's talents, do exciting work, and make an impact on the world around us. Join us and redefine your personal best.


About Stantec


Stantec is a global leader in sustainable engineering, architecture, and environmental consulting. The diverse perspectives of our partners and interested parties drive us to think beyond what’s previously been done on critical issues like climate change, digital transformation, and future\-proofing our cities and infrastructure. We innovate at the intersection of community, creativity, and client relationships to advance communities everywhere, so that together we can redefine what’s possible.


**Pay Transparency:** In compliance with pay transparency laws, pay ranges are provided for positions in locations where required. Please note, the final agreed upon compensation is based on individual education, qualifications, experience, and work location. At Stantec certain roles are bonus eligible. Actual compensation for part\-time roles will be pro\-rated based on the agreed number of working hours per week.


**Benefits Summary:** Regular full\-time and part\-time employees (working at least 20 hours per week) will have access to health, dental, and vision plans, a wellness program, health care spending account, wellness spending account, group registered retirement savings plan, employee stock purchase program, group tax\-free savings account, life and accidental death \& dismemberment (AD\&D) insurance, short\-term/long\-term disability plans, emergency travel benefits, tuition reimbursement, professional membership fee coverage, and paid time off.


Temporary/casual employees will have access to group registered retirement savings plan, employee stock purchase program, and group tax\-free savings account.


The benefits information listed above may not apply to union positions because benefits for such positions are governed by applicable collective bargaining agreements.


**Primary Location:** Canada \| MB \| Winnipeg


**Organization:** BC\-1137 Transpt\-CA Manitoba


**Employee Status:** Regular


**Business Justification:** New Position


**Travel:** Yes


**Schedule:** Full time

The Senior Project Manager applies established project management principles, tools, and templates including the executing, controlling and closing phases. Provides leadership to ensure the overall completion of project deliverables are achieved including managing project scope, resources, communications, risks/issues, budget, timelines, procurement, integration, quality and implementation. title: PROJECT MANAGEMENT COE DEPT MANAGER
description:
**Project Management CoE Department Manager**


**Winnipeg, MB**  

**Manitoba Hydro is consistently recognized as one of Manitoba's Top Employers!** We are a leader among energy companies in  

North America, recognized for providing highly reliable service and exceptional customer satisfaction. Join our team of Manitoba's  

best as we continue to build a company that champions safety, supports innovation, and delivers on our commitment to customer  

service \- while actively fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace reflective of the communities we serve.


***Great Benefits***


* Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
* Defined\-benefit pension plan for long\-term financial security.
* Nine\-day work cycle, typically resulting in every other Monday off to support a balanced approach to work, family life and



community.


***Position Overview:***



We are seeking a Project Management Centre of Expertise Department Manager to join lead the Project Management Centre of  

Expertise Department. Reporting to the Director of Project Management, will provide strategic leadership over the development and  

implementation of Asset Planning \& Delivery's Project Management Centre of Expertise to support and enhance the enterprise's  

ability to meet strategic, operational, and financial objectives. This position plays a key role in leading and elevating Project  

Management delivery by collaboratively establishing, promoting, applying, and improving Project Management best practices within  

the business unit and enterprise. This includes the delivery of enterprise projects, providing contracts \& claims services, establishing  

project management standards, procedures and governance, integrating project safety and Indigenous reconciliation into every  

stage of the project lifecycle.


***Responsibilities:***


* Provide leadership to the Project Management Division working closely with all Managers to advance the practice of project


management, lead by example with the delivery of enterprise projects and drive a ONE project team culture.
* Develop short\-term and long\-term plans to ensure that the Project Management Centre of Expertise reflects Manitoba Hydro's


strategic and business priorities and supports the Division to deliver the capital project portfolio in collaboration with Asset  

Management Division.
* Promote and advocate for the collaborative practice, integration and continuous improvement of project safety throughout the


project lifecycle with project managers and leaders throughout the business unit and including managing contractor safety.
* Develop tools to enable the Division and BU to efficiently execute the portfolio of approved projects.
* Track the maturity of the enterprise project management capability by regularly assessing the capability and incorporate



feedback/current best practices to ensure continuous improvement.  

* Mature the project management system and capability across the organization by sharing best practices, and by building


project management skillsets of other departments/divisions including the development and delivery of PM training.  

* Establish enterprise\-wide standards, guidelines, and project governance to optimize the delivery of projects.
* Advance the practice of contract/claims management in collaboration with other AP\&D Divisions.
* Responsible for the development of performance management standards for project managers by defining clear objectives,


and assessing them against those objectives, and providing continuous feedback to improve performance.  

* Monitor compliance with the project management standards.
* Develop and maintain key performance indicators for the portfolio and the PM Division.

***Qualifications:***


* Completion of an Engineering degree from an institute of recognized standing in applicable discipline plus nine years of


related experience demonstrating progressively greater responsibility in a supervisory/leadership capacity.  

Professional member in good standing with Engineers Geoscientists Manitoba (or willingness and ability to attain within a  

specified amount of time).

MANITOBA HYDRO IS COMMITTED TO DIVERSITY AND EMPLOYMENT EQUITY


 Reference Code: CO57184877\-01



OR  

* Equivalent degree and professional designation in a related field relevant to project management will be considered.
* Project Management Professional (PMP) designation is considered an asset.
* Demonstrated ability to provide strategic leadership in planning, organizing, and managing multiple activities within strict


timelines.  

* Superior interpersonal and communication skills; with the ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with


management and staff throughout the enterprise.  

* An ability to work effectively with a broad network of internal and external stakeholders.
* Experience in strategic planning and business unit planning activities

***Salary Range***



Starting salary will be commensurate with qualifications and experience. The range for the classification is $62\.82\-$86\.07 Hourly,  

$120,372\.72\-$164,936\.72 Annually.


***Apply Now!***



Ready to join a team that energizes Manitoba and puts safety, innovation, and inclusion at the heart of everything we do? Visit  

www.hydro.mb.ca/careers to learn more about this position and to apply online.


**Application deadline: APRIL 14, 2026\.**

process will be contacted.

description:

=======================


Apply now »
**Date:** Mar 18, 2026

**Company:** Magellan


Magellan Aerospace is a global, integrated aerospace company that provides complex assemblies and systems solutions to aircraft and engine manufacturers, and defence and space agencies worldwide. At Magellan we have over 80 years of collective knowledge and experience in the aerospace industry, and believe one truth: People create value.

  




Employees are our greatest resource in working towards a successful future, and together we build professional relationships that thrive. We know that personal development is fundamental to the success of every individual and invest in our employees.

  




When you are part of Magellan, you are part of the team!

**Position Summary**

  



Magellan Aerospace has an exciting opportunity for an **SAP Project Manager.** Reporting into senior management, the successful incumbent will be key in delivering the successful execution of Magellan’s global business transformation. The most significant delivery will be the S/4HANA deployment across our global divisions. This role will be responsible for ensuring the project remains on plan, on budget and meets the aims of the business. In this role, the SAP Project Manager will collaborate with SAP functional, technical, development and architecture teams, as well as other key business resources.

  


**Essential Responsibilities**

  


* On\-time delivery of program commitments is key, and this role will be responsible for implementing
* Program metrics and managing associated progress reporting.
* Lead project day\-to\-day execution, facilitate communication and drive progress against plans
* Track, manage, prioritize and escalate project issues and risks
* As appropriate, set priorities for the team to ensure task completion and coordinate work activities
* Support the governance process will also be a key component of the role
* Provide Project Management support to a cross\-functional program team delivering an SAP
* S/4HANA implementation project
* Ownership for ensuring a robust project plan is in place and maintained accurately
* Work closely with both internal and third\-party teams
* Create and maintain effective relationships with partner organizations where required
* Provide weekly status reports to the project sponsors
* Improve internal company knowledge in software project management and delivery

  


**Position Requirements**

  


* Bachelor's Degree or equivalent in a relevant discipline
* PMP or Prince2 certification is desirable
* 4\+ years working as a project manager in a leading ERP system, with preferably experience in at least 1 full SAP S/4HANA implementation project
* Proven track record in project delivery for large, highly complex, integrated application landscapes of large global transformation programs
* Good knowledge of Software Development Lifecycle
* Experience of working in large programs of work with multi\-location teams
* Flexible to adapt to working in a lean environment
* Ability to build good relationships both within own team and with other teams

* Flexibility to adjust to multiple demands, shifting priorities, ambiguity and rapid change
* Ability to deal efficiently with escalations and difficult situations/people under pressure

* Extensive experience working with 3rd party vendors as service providers
* Demonstrated experience in change management and cross functional team leadership as well as exceptional communication and presentation skills
* Good interpersonal skills liaising with internal and external contacts
* Team Player – able to collaborate with colleagues
* Able to Multi\-task and work under tight deadlines
* 20\-30% travel required

  


**Why Magellan?**

  


* Competitive salary
* Attractive benefits package (fully Company paid)
* Generous Company pension plan
* Positive work\-life balance
* Excellent career progression opportunities
* Employee Assistance Program
* Free parking
